author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
@SW4GN3R 

During the EOY Townhall in December, you shared that CORE Act 3 would launch soon, withits first issue going live alongside (or shortly after) Holosim Chapter 2 would launch. The idea being that we could earn the first issue of the Act in-game, I believe.

Chapter 2 took a bit longer to release than anticipated, but it's been live for a few weeks now, and I was wondering what the current plan is for the launch of the third and final Graphic Novel Act.

I sincerely hope Tim is still with the company, or otherwise at least finished the project before he was let go

author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content: Circulating supply is going to be pretty limited. However, the inflation rate is highest in the first two years -- pretty significant exponential decay function on inflation -- which should incentivize delegating stake. Also, people that want to run a validator will need to buy tokens to do so

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Do you really think the priority of this document is low?  I genuinely ask this because, of the people I've talked to in the last 5 years that I've been part of the community and asked "what is the main reason you decided to participate in the project?", 3 out of 5 answer me "the incredible whitepaper" or "the detailed tokenonomics".  The BTC today is only what it is because it started with the initial manifesto. I may be a bit old-fashioned, but a good document (even if it doesn't cover 100% of what the project aims to do) would greatly help in the growth and dissemination of the network.

author: [COEX] 𝙈𝙪𝙖𝙙'𝙙𝙞𝙗
content:
From a conversation between me and my friend:

Unless they implement a zero-tolerance policy and ban alts from the ground up, this game is fundamentally DOA. They shouldn't just target alts; main accounts need to be nuked as well. Without firm resolve, the game will quickly devolve into a lawless free-for-all. No reputable RTS in the world ignores or sanctions multi-accounting. Even without a direct mechanical edge, indirect advantages shatter the game's balance. Restricting inter-account trading is a mere facade; in practice, indirect benefits still trickle through. Besides, this game is built on Peer-to-Peer (P2P) trade. How far can you realistically restrict that? In your economy-driven version, the entire system is built on this very pillar.

This environment is a blatant injustice to single-account players. Professional schedule aside, even with infinite leisure, why should I be forced to manage 4-5 accounts? It’s an absurd design flaw. They are cornering legitimate players into multi-accounting just to stay competitive. To any outsider, the verdict is immediate: The fish rots from the head. If developers turn a blind eye to multi-accounting, the project is fundamentally compromised and unplayable.

Another issue is systemic imbalance. Beyond multi-accounting’s damage, we see massive over-concentration in specific factions. USTUR has been left completely isolated. As an ONI player, I wouldn't want to be an USTUR right now. What was the rationale behind stripping LP (Loyalty Points) from core mechanics? Wasn’t the LP system meant to check-and-balance faction populations? Don’t take this wrong, but your game concept is solid while the execution and mentality are flawed. You have the right product, but the wrong vision.
